curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: When you're out there, nobody cares how it felt. They only care what you did.
quotes.speaker: Rocky (1976)
quotes.context: Mickey talking to Rocky during a training session
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Mickey's gruff, unsentimental coaching voice is well-matched here, and the sentiment fits his no-excuses philosophy. The subtle giveaway is it leans slightly aphoristic; Mickey's real lines are usually more insult-forward and specific to Rocky's failings.

quotes.text: Every man on this beach is somebody's son. You'd do well to remember that.
quotes.speaker: Saving Private Ryan (1998)
quotes.context: A sergeant addressing younger soldiers after the Normandy landing
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the moral weight and clipped soldier-speak fit Spielberg's film perfectly, and that kind of reminder-of-humanity line recurs in war pictures. The giveaway is that it's a little more rhetorically shaped than the film's ragged, exhausted dialogue usually allows.

quotes.text: It appears my hypocrisy knows no bounds.
quotes.speaker: Tombstone (1993)
quotes.context: Doc Holliday (Val Kilmer), the consumptive gambler-gunslinger, wryly acknowledging his own contradictions.

quotes.text: You want to know what I think? I think you already knew the answer before you walked in here.
quotes.speaker: Chinatown (1974)
quotes.context: Jake Gittes, pressing a client who is holding something back
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Gittes is exactly this kind of weary, cornering talker, and the line fits his interrogation style cold. The subtle giveaway is that it's a touch too tidy a reversal; Towne's real dialogue tends to bury the insight in more clutter.

quotes.text: He hasn't been right since Korea. Maybe before that.
quotes.speaker: The Deer Hunter (1978)
quotes.context: A character in Clairton making an aside about a troubled older man
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the film is saturated with this kind of low-key, community-gossip observation about men damaged by war, and the cadence is exactly right for its western Pennsylvania working-class world. The giveaway is that nobody in the film actually references Korea; Vietnam is the wound.

quotes.text: Strange women lying in ponds distributing swords is no basis for a system of government.
quotes.speaker: Monty Python and the Holy Grail (1975)
quotes.context: Dennis the peasant (Michael Palin) argues political theory with King Arthur about the Lady of the Lake.

source.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail - Wikiquote
